intro.value: This transition is the mean-field reduction step. It shows how the finite-N order parameter in Eq. 2 collapses the pairwise interaction sum into one collective pull.
The only extra assumption is uniform all-to-all coupling, K sub i j equals K over N. Once that is imposed, Eq. 2 supplies exactly the complex average needed to rewrite the interaction term.

steps.rightTitle: Start from Eq. 1 and Specialize the Coupling

left.value: Eq. 1 is still the general pairwise model. To reach the standard Kuramoto equation, replace the full coupling matrix by the mean-field choice K sub i j equals K over N.
That turns the interaction term into one global average over all oscillators instead of a separate coefficient for each pair.

steps.rightTitle: Use the Order Parameter from Eq. 2

left.value: Eq. 2 packages the phase population into one complex average. Multiply it by e to the minus i theta sub i so the phase of oscillator i is measured relative to the collective phase.
That produces exactly the shifted phase factors appearing inside the interaction sum.
The interaction in Eq. 1 is built from sines, so now take the imaginary part of the complex identity.
This is the exact algebraic move that turns the order parameter into the scalar mean-field force.
Put the reduced interaction term back into the specialized equation from Step 1.
At that point the full pairwise sum has disappeared, and the dynamics are expressed through one self-consistent collective field.
